description_long: This temple is constructed from light-colored stone blocks and features a rectangular layout with a prominent columned portico at the front. The entrance is framed by four tall, fluted columns with ornate capitals, supporting a flat entablature and roof. The roof itself is flat and bordered by a raised parapet, with two large rectangular openings visible. Along one side, a row of additional columns stands, some of which are freestanding and others attached to the main structure. The stonework is uniform, with minimal surface ornamentation, emphasizing the geometric clarity of the architecture. The overall design evokes classical Greco-Roman temple forms, with a focus on symmetry, proportion, and monumental presence.
description: A light stone temple with a columned portico, flat roof, and classical Greco-Roman architectural features. The structure is rectangular, with prominent columns and minimal exterior ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with four tall columns and a deep portico leading into the temple.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the full length of the temple, a row of columns along the side, and the flat roof with rectangular openings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid stone back wall and the raised parapet of the flat roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the side wall and the arrangement of columns at the front and along the side.
attribute_ornamentation: minimalist with some column capital detailing
attribute_entrance_design: Columned portico with four tall fluted columns, rectangular doorway, and simple entablature
